text:
2 years out: The meta-interface revolution!

Devices will take a while to catch up, due to hardware constraints and the complexity of designing an OS. expect radically different mobile devices (possibly laptops too) 2 years from now. 

they won't be the same kind of device. it used to be about being a telephone, for calling people. now it will be about... i really don't know. some kind of entertainment/creation/AI communication device. no screen? foldable large screen? 3D projection? some kinda VR/AR thing? idk, it's anyone's game. the interface will find us, we'll start to see the specialization of certain interfaces for the common use cases.

text:
Copied from email i wrote to a friend: 
I'm amazed and enthused with the fact that at the Prospera explainer event in Aevitas House in Dec 2024, I wasn't even thinking about AI timelines drastically affecting longevity. But now? June 2025? Obviously our lifespans are going to be seriously extended. And i hate to say longevity escape velocity is true bc it defies my conventional wisdom so hard but it would be absurd if our extended lifespans dont make room for hyper-extreme life extension which itself makes room for possible actual immortality. The error bars are huge but it's ludicrous not to imagine it assuming quantum, fusion, etc. Seriously a WTF moment. Life is gonna change drastically and I just saw a freaking Adam Ragusea vid where he says "LLMs just ingest our writing and extrude it back to us and charge us a monthly fee for the pleasure". No one gets it! It's not priced in!!!

text:
Does this mean we are 16% to AGI?
Maybe. The benchmark aims to be directionally correct—it measures capacities that are “necessary” for AGI but not necessarily “sufficient”. 

ARC-AGI creator says we may see a model that is decidedly not AGI get 100% on ARC-AGI-2.

text:
Why is there something rather than nothing? #PrimeMover

How come we’re both conscious but we have no direct access to each others’ perceptions? #PhenomenalBindingProblem

Btw your world is generated by your brain. Lucid dreams are generated by your brain—life works the same way, predictive processing. You dont have direct access to “reality”. The objective world not only “looks different” than you think it does—it necessarily *doesnt LOOK like anything*. #IndirectRealism

The world only has an ability to LOOK like something by virtue of your subjective (biased) perspective. There IS NO UNBIASED PERSPECTIVE. The world doesnt look, feel, sound, smell, or taste like ANYTHING. Your entire world is a series of fabrications constructed inside your brain. So choose the see the world in a way that is most in harmony with all that is. #SeeingThatFrees

Soo… whats actually out there? 👀 #epistemology #ontology

text:
AI summarizing my soobstack post:

The post is a practical philosophy guide inspired by EEAAO, urging “multiverse surfing” through unlikely actions, intentional decision-making, kindness, and grounding in the present.

Telos Post Summary

This essay extracts “psychotechnologies” from Everything Everywhere All at Once to help you act with agency. The core idea is using parallel universes as mental tools: create “gateway events” by doing unlikely things to unlock new possibilities, meet “gateway people,” and place yourself in “gateway universes” where versions of you have skills you can channel. Through “counter‑factual imagineering,” visualize alternate selves to dissolve “I can’t,” borrow their paths, and commit to trade‑offs.

It then tackles constraints like time scarcity, addictions, brain limits (neural annealing), anxiety/attachment, and nervous‑system overload. Decision strategies include an internal congress of parts (IFS), aligning across selves via shared Schelling points, branching decision trees, and tactics to recover “multiversal slack” (graceful no’s, frugality, allowing unknown unknowns, mindful time dilation—with caution around potent psychedelics like 5‑MeO‑DMT).

On meaning, it narrates a progression from tradition to nihilism to meta‑ironic meaning, with Evelyn choosing care—especially for her daughter—as a self-grounding source. Ethically, it centers Waymond’s principle: be kind when you don’t know what’s going on, operationalized through metta, NVC, and embodied practices (bodyscan, Vipassana, Focusing, Alexander Technique).

Finally, it argues for “finding the others”—people who share unusual consciousness experiences—to be truly seen, and closes with a checklist: leverage gateways, decide multiversally, be kind, stay in the body, and don’t surf alone.

Through “counter‑factual imagineering,” visualize alternate selves to dissolve “I can’t,” borrow their paths, and commit to trade‑offs.

It then tackles constraints like time scarcity, addictions, brain limits (neural annealing), anxiety/attachment, and nervous‑system overload. Decision strategies include an internal congress of parts (IFS), aligning across selves via shared Schelling points, branching decision trees, and tactics to recover “multiversal slack” (graceful no’s, frugality, allowing unknown unknowns, mindful time dilation—with caution around potent psychedelics like 5‑MeO‑DMT).

On meaning, it narrates a progression from tradition to nihilism to meta‑ironic meaning, with Evelyn choosing care—especially for her daughter—as a self-grounding source. Ethically, it centers Waymond’s principle: be kind when you don’t know what’s going on, operationalized through metta, NVC, and embodied practices (bodyscan, Vipassana, Focusing, Alexander Technique).

Finally, it argues for “finding the others”—people who share unusual consciousness experiences—to be truly seen, and closes with a checklist: leverage gateways, decide multiversally, be kind, stay in the body, and don’t surf alone.

Welp.. sister just had her “You Lack the Vitamin” moment.

co2 level in bedroom: 3100+ ppm(!) 

🟢 <1000 ppm — Fresh, normal indoor air
🟡 1000-2000 ppm — Stuffy, drowsiness & cognitive impairment begins
🟠 2000-3000 ppm — Headaches, poor focus, fatigue, slower reaction times
🔴 3000-5000 ppm — Significant cognitive impairment, brain fog, poor sleep, increased heart rate
☠️ 5000+ ppm — OSHA limit, dizziness, increasingly elevated heart rate, unsafe

text:
I'm going to do something crazy tonight.
I will probably black out.
I'm going to alter my brainwaves to an entirely different frequency.
I'm going to go full Wolverine Mode and autophagize my body's old cells, healing all organ systems at an incredible rate.
Tonight, I'm going to relax so hard that my brain will literally stop encoding new memories. In fact, ALL of my memories will feel slightly different after.
In 8 or so hours I will come to, with ~NO recollection of the elapsed time. And I'll feel somehow emotionally rejuvenated and more ready than ever for the challenges ahead.
Tonight, I'm going to bed. 🌙

text:
Today is March 1st, 2026. GPT 3.5 Turbo was released 3 years ago this month.

I don't think I quite realized that first time I saw ChatGPT 3.5, that I was seeing the 'before times' for the last time.

My perspective at the time: "Wow! What a neat technology! So many applications!" 

I didn't realize that it wasn't a product to be applied to things but rather a substrate that would fundamentally change the nature of what a product even is. Let alone so soon.

text: Giving users what they want is how we got into this mess. The products of the future will be what users needed the whole time. The signal will overcome the noise as the cost of product development approaches zero, and bespoke software products proliferate. What makes a product stand out won't be that it's addictive, but that it's genuinely useful.

What makes a product stand out won't be that it's addictive, but that it's genuinely useful.

text:
why 5-MeO-DMT works 
(@bryan_johnson, this take is why I recommended you have @algekalipso on during your experience):

"An aspect of 5-MeO-DMT phenomenology that gives the "I became God" impression is the loss of embodied personality metadata in the rendering of one's experience. My impression is that we hold a sense of who we are in the shape of muscle tension, especially our face, which provides the main affective and identity channel for social communication, and that 5-MeO-DMT radically reorganizes those patterns. In particular, it creates large patches of expansion/contraction rather than the very surgical and microexpression-aware identity construction we usually carry around implicitly in the background. We can code our experiences as "ours" in part because the presence of this social identity plays the role of the witness of the experience for holographic encoding. When the social mask is dissolved through large blunt patches of expansion and contraction, who is the one who experiences this? Of course, your face was never the witness, but that has been an integral part of the social software we always live with, so its sudden breakdown causes a powerful impression of conscious anonymity."

text:
So goddamn excited about open-source. Open sourcing is really important because:
- You plug into the collaborative ecosystem by building tools that are modular and fit-to-form.
- You spark others' imagination by providing cognitive scaffolding
- You are harvesting alpha building products that people love
- You are not stifling innovation due to your ego and some megalomaniacal obsession with capturing >100% of the value you create. (the #1 Silicon Valley critique)

Open-source is the future--if you want to exist in the age of $0 software development, you have to be open-source. AGI lives and breathes on open source. Yes!! You must Open-source Yourself!!

What I've taken to is just aggressive management of my session context:
- one-time /statusline for persistent context awareness, not waiting for Anthropic to tell me to /clear bc by then I'm alr hemmoraghing tokens
- /clear early and often, pass context manually to fresh sesh
- subagents to pare down the context for a particular subtask so I don't have to break flow with /clear

This lets me run my ~10 parallel claudes on Max x20 :L)

text:
“I guess thats just what it costs” is a mental motion I find myself increasingly rejecting now, out of necessity. You should never accept it. Reduce spending. Create homemade alternatives. Share them with others. “We have abundance at home”. Someone, somewhere, is extorting you. Reject the inherited supply chain and its middle-man tax, which benefits no one. Build your own furniture. Radical self-sufficiency is a rebelliously pro-human act.

Create for yourself, share for others, inspire the rest.

text:
my personal takeaways from Vitalik's Galaxy Brain Resistance article:

- "give me power because I'm more ethical" doesn't make you ethical. you have to prove it, over and over again.
- The ends don't justify the means: Hold strong principles and tell people about them. I'm perturbed and frankly disgusted by some of the power-seekers I've gotten to know in the Bay Area, and refuse to play their game. I'm principled and aim to more clearly vocalize what those principles are. I want people to know what I stand for.
- Align the incentives: Your success should be aligned with the success of those who are rooting for you, and never at their expense.

source article link: https://t.co/R73RW6r3Rl

text:
My mission: choosing Aliveness over Numbness.
My vision: thriving 🌱.
My anti-vision: decay 🏚️.
My community: here (🐦), discord, substack, group chats, DMs (which are open).

If you're reading this, you're probably more like a person who stops cycles of intergenerational bullshit, than one who perpetuates it. Props to you! Though you may still be wondering why I care so much about nourishment and flourishing, so here's my story in brief:

Early life circumstances arrested my development, physically, emotionally, and morally. I know what 'failure to thrive' feels like from the inside, and I fought that each day I chose painful aliveness out of painless numbness. In this process of learning how to be higher-aliveness, I found myself face-to-face with humanity's nastiest systemic issues and became dedicated to living a life in service to humanity, since many of our issues share the same systemic causes. I am now officially on this mission to build better systems that change our default from numbness to aliveness. I invite you to subscribe to me here + on Substack to become a part of this story.

text:
Some key aspects to consider:
- labs can't benchmaxx on a benchmark they don't know about. creating your own benchmarks will always be more productive for you personally.
- you want an AI with the right set of mental heuristics, and without sloppy mental tendencies (like conflation).
- personality, and behavior, varies between AI. You want an AI that understands what you mean when you under-specify.
- taste (and its opposite: slop) is an underrated factor in LLM selection

Cars spend 96% of their time parked. 

Autonomous cars, specifically self-driving fleets for communal use, will be the future of transportation for as long as we continue to rely on roads. They kill 5x fewer people than human drivers and don't need breaks.

Waymo currently sits at 30% utilization (time with a passenger)--this is 7.5x higher than personal cars already. That number will only go up as the infrastructure scales.

And you ask why I don't believe in car ownership! I only wish robotaxis were already 3x cheaper.

text:
Part of why this bugs me so much is because Silicon Valley has meant so much to me—i believe in innovation. The world is stupid in so many ways and sv is great at calling that out.

Which is why I’m calling Garry out.

When the CEO of supporting silicon valley dreamers can’t bring himself to write his own articles, it makes all of us look bad, and puts a ceiling on our potential.

If anything, this has inspired me to lean harder into writing. If the CEO of YC is writing AI slop and getting thousands of likes, I have legitimate value to contribute by writing Human Words.

I hope Garry takes this criticism in the spirit it’s intended: looking at his output from first-principles and thinking “it doesn’t have to be like this”.

text:
For the record, I wonder if this council (or panel) concept has legs, not in terms of throwing more LLMs at the problem, but less--specifically in terms of neurosymbolic augmentation of AI. 

Can you make an AI smarter by implementing a fact-checker that examines implicit claims made by the AI, making them explicit and adding challenges or 'requests for evidence' into the models' thinking chain? A logic buddy, if you will. 

cc @distbit0 since we've talked about this

text:
Yesterday, while trying to find if the big red writing book I had been clutching in my dream that night existed, I found a phrase from David Foster Wallace that impacted me: "the audience can't read your mind."

Today, I wrote the words on paper "you can just understand things", feeling ready to share this profundity with the world, until I realized it was hardly clear. You can’t read my mind, so let me attempt to convey the personal transformation this insight is causing me:

You can just dig into things you don't understand until you do understand them, because that's important, because without understanding, we're in a world where all is taken for granted: a world of stuff designed by people capital-s Smarter than us, which we have no ability to control, influence, or impact, which lies so far beyond our grasp that we might as well be the cosmic equivalent of pets roaming around in an impenetrable world, existing for someone else's amusement or comfort.

No, the world is penetrable, navigable, with handles that are grabbable and manipulable. When Jobs said the world was “designed by people no Smarter than you”, he was using the capital ‘S’. As in not leagues different; you are the same beast that created this beautiful mess, and you can change the world through not passively existing in it, but actively seeking to understand it, letting your deep experience of it change you so that you can change it back. 

I’m on a learning journey and it’s causing me a personal blossoming. I’m realizing you can just understand things, and it’s making me really happy. 

This feeling of understandability is what civilization has set us for, and also how new civilizations are founded. What did America’s Founding Fathers understand about society and human nature? Maybe you can understand that, too, and create a better world from first principles than the one you grew up experiencing. The greatest thinkers were no Smarter than you. All it takes to start changing the world for the better is asking good questions.
